help.heading: ❓ How to use the dashboard
help.subtitle: A quick guide to running standups with JIRA Crystal Ball.

fortune.heading: 🔮 Fortune — Draft a ticket with Gemini
fortune.subtitle: Drop files or paste a requirement. Gemini drafts a JIRA ticket in Gherkin UAC form. Review, then create it on your squad's board.
fortune.mock: UI preview — generation & creation are mocked until backend wiring is approved.
fortune.singleDesc: One ticket (Story/Task/Bug/Spike) with full Gherkin User Acceptance Criteria.
fortune.epicDesc: Split into one Epic + linked child issues, each with full UAC.
fortune.sourceHint: Attach files (text, image, or PDF), type directly, or both — everything is sent to Gemini.
fortune.dropTitle: Drag & drop files here, or click to choose
fortune.dropSub: Text/Markdown, images (PNG/JPG), or PDF — max 15MB each
fortune.textPlaceholderSingle: e.g. As a user I want to reset my password via email…
fortune.textPlaceholderEpic: e.g. Payment module: gateway integration, transaction history, refunds…
fortune.generatingHint: Reading your input and writing Gherkin-style UAC.
fortune.reviewReady: Draft ready. Review & edit, then create the ticket.
fortune.needInput: Enter requirement text or attach at least one file first.
fortune.confirmBoard: This ticket will be created on board {{board}} (squad {{squad}}).
fortune.confirmNoBoard: Squad “{{squad}}” has no default board set — configure it in Settings first.
fortune.confirmNotConfigured: JIRA is not configured for squad “{{squad}}”. An admin must set credentials in the server .env.
fortune.reporterHint: The ticket's reporter is resolved from this JIRA user's email (accountId).
fortune.cantDelete: Only the creator or a super admin can delete this draft.
fortune.tip1b: Mix requirement text with screenshots or a PDF — Gemini reads them all.
fortune.tip2b: Drafts come as GIVEN / WHEN / THEN UAC. Edit anything before creating.
fortune.tip3b: Not quite right? Send a follow-up prompt and Gemini rewrites the draft.
fortune.tip4b: Use a real JIRA user's email — resolved to an accountId at create time.
fortune.tip5b: After creating, replace every [image-…] placeholder with the real Figma/screenshot.
fortune.tip6b: Set assignee, story points, sprint, components & links directly on the ticket.
fortune.tip7b: Open a draft to continue editing where you left off. “Start over” discards the current draft and begins a fresh one.
fortune.tip8b: Replace the TBD links in ## Description with the real Figma, PRD/Confluence, Postman & API path — before or right after creating.
fortune.refineHint: Describe what to change; Gemini rewrites the current draft.
fortune.refinePlaceholder: e.g. Add an edge case for expired tokens; make the copy Indonesian…
fortune.next1: Open the ticket and replace every [image-or-design-ui-from-figma] with the real design/screenshot.
fortune.next2: Fill in the Description links (Figma, PRD/Confluence, Postman, API path) left as TBD.
fortune.next3: Complete JIRA fields: assignee, story points, sprint, components, labels & issue links.
fortune.next4: Review the Gherkin UAC with the team before starting work.
fortune.recreateHint: Opens in the edit view first — review & tweak, then create again.
fortune.ctxHint: The requirement + refine turns sent to Gemini — kept so refine keeps working after Recreate.
fortune.usageEst: Tokens are real; cost is estimated — actual billing lives in Google Cloud Console.
fortune.saveModalHint: Update the draft you opened, or keep it and save this as a separate new draft.
fortune.startOverBody: This clears the current draft and its context. Save it as a draft first if you want to keep it.
fortune.importDesc: Import a ticket from your board, refine its description & info with Gemini, then update it.
fortune.importSearchHint: Search a ticket on this squad's board by key or title, then pick it to import.
fortune.importNoMatch: No matching tickets on the board.
fortune.confirmUpdate: Update {{key}} on board {{board}} with your edits?
fortune.undoHint: Restores the ticket's values from before this update.

dashboard.jiraModalBody: JIRA credentials are not set on the server. An admin must set JIRA_BASE_URL, JIRA_EMAIL and JIRA_API_TOKEN in the server .env and restart. You can still record standup updates manually — only live ticket data needs JIRA.
dashboard.noActiveOnBoard: No active sprint found on the board.
dashboard.createSprintHint: Create a sprint to start recording standup updates.

board.title: Board — Active Sprint Tickets
board.subtitle: All tickets in the board's currently active sprint, live from JIRA.
board.jiraNotConfigured: JIRA is not configured for this squad. Set it up in Settings.
board.hiddenByDefaultHint: Done / Archived are hidden by default. Click a status to toggle.
board.noTickets: No tickets match the current filter.

notFound.message: The crystal ball sees no page here.

previous.confluenceNotConfigured: Confluence not configured on the server.
previous.noStandupEntries: No standup entries recorded for this sprint.
previous.carryOverTooltip: Tickets not yet Done (In QA + In Progress + To Do). These are expected to roll over (carry over) into the next sprint.

comp.filterSummary: ({{shown}}/{{total}}) · Done/Archived hidden by default · Enter / Alt+↑↓ to move between rows
comp.noTickets: No tickets. Check JIRA config in Settings, or board may be empty.
comp.noTicketsMatchFilter: No tickets match the current status filter.
comp.carryOverSeen: Carry-over — seen in an earlier sprint
comp.boardIdHelpAfter: , or a project key). Leave blank to use the env default
comp.boardIdHelpEnd: — board ticket views just stay empty if no board id is resolvable.
comp.holdHint: Put on hold (blocked / deprioritized) — greys out the row

settings.name: Name
settings.adminAccountsDesc: Only you (the environment super admin) can add, edit, delete, or reset passwords for admin accounts. The super admin account itself cannot be modified here.
settings.deleteAdminMessage: Delete admin {{email}}? This cannot be undone.
settings.dangerZoneDesc: Deletes all squads and everything under them (members, leaves, holidays, sprints, standup entries, blockers, JIRA configs). User accounts are kept. This cannot be undone.
settings.reseedDefaults: Recreate default squads (Athens / Berlin / Cairo) after reset
settings.resetDatabaseMessage: This permanently deletes ALL squads and their data (members, leaves, holidays, sprints, standup entries, blockers, JIRA configs). User accounts are kept. This cannot be undone.
settings.deleteSquadMessage: Delete squad "{{label}}"? All its sprints, members, blockers and JIRA config are removed. This cannot be undone.
settings.spFieldHelp: Story Points field — id (e.g. customfield_10033) or exact field name (e.g. "Story Points QA").
settings.confluenceHelp: Confluence export target — leave blank to use the global env defaults (CONFLUENCE_SPACE_KEY / CONFLUENCE_PARENT_ID).
settings.tarotScaleHelp: Tarot (planning poker) default scale — used when a host opens a new room (the host can still override per-room). ? and ☕ cards are always added.
settings.jiraUserPlaceholder: pick a name or paste an account id
settings.geminiDesc: Controls how the Fortune ticket creator generates text. Applies to all squads.
settings.geminiNotConfigured: GEMINI_API_KEY is not set on the server — Fortune generation stays disabled until it's configured.
settings.geminiRange: Temperature must be a number between 0 and 2.
settings.seedDesc: Paste or upload a JSON config to create/update squads and team members (idempotent — safe to re-run). Uploaded files are read in your browser and not stored.

panels.teamNoMembers: No members yet. Add them in Settings.
panels.leadNoMembers: Add team members to build the rotation.
panels.summaryCarryOverTooltip: Tickets not yet Done (In QA + In Progress + To Do). These are expected to roll over (carry over) into the next sprint.

velocity.heading: 📈 Velocity & Burndown
velocity.noSprintData: No sprint data yet. Story points come from standup ticket snapshots — run some standups first.
velocity.barChartHint: Hover a sprint for details · click to see its burndown below.
velocity.noDailyData: No daily data for this sprint.
velocity.burndownHint: Hover the line for each day’s numbers.
velocity.tip1Title: Commit to what you can finish
velocity.tip1Body: A healthy velocity is one you can hit consistently. Plan each sprint around your recent average completed points — not your best-ever sprint. Over-committing just grows carry-over.
velocity.tip2Body: The ideal line is a straight drop to zero. If your remaining line stays flat early and crashes at the end, work is finishing too late — split large tickets and close them across the sprint.
velocity.tip3Body: Smaller tickets (1–3 pts) burn down smoothly and are easier to estimate. Epics that sit at ‘in progress’ for days hide risk. Aim for tickets a person can finish in a day or two.
velocity.tip4Body: Velocity only means something if a ‘5’ means the same thing every sprint. Re-anchor with the team in Tarot planning so estimates don’t drift over time.
velocity.tip5Body: A blocked ticket freezes its points on the burndown. Surface blockers in standup the day they appear, not at sprint end — that’s the single biggest lever on completed velocity.
velocity.tip6Body: One low sprint isn’t a problem; a downward trend is. Watch the bar chart across sprints — a stable or gently rising completed line is the goal, not spikes.
velocity.sourceStandupHint: Computed from this tool's standup snapshots (story points + status recorded each day). Fills in as you run standups.
velocity.sourceJiraHint: Computed live from JIRA closed sprints (board story points). No standups needed; burndown is not available for this source.
velocity.noJiraData: No closed sprints with story points found on the JIRA board.

clairvoyance.heading: 👁️ Clairvoyance — Grooming
clairvoyance.subtitle: Future-sprint & backlog tickets, live from JIRA. Pick a source to groom.
clairvoyance.jiraNotConfigured: JIRA is not configured for this squad. Set it up in Settings.
clairvoyance.emptyState: No future sprints or backlog tickets found. Create them in JIRA, then reload.

helpSections.gettingStartedItem1: Pick your squad from the dropdown in the header. Each squad has its own board, members, sprints and holidays.
helpSections.gettingStartedItem2: Guests run standups without an account; admins (login) also manage Settings. On the guest screen the name field suggests existing team-member names as you type.
helpSections.gettingStartedItem3: Need the current sprint pulled in? Use ↻ Sync from JIRA on the Current Sprint page.
helpSections.standupItem1: Click ▶ Start standup to take the lead — only you can edit while you lead; others are read-only. Click ■ End standup to release (it also auto-releases if you close the tab).
helpSections.standupItem2: Pick the Standup date (within the sprint range) or click a day in the Sprint Timeline.
helpSections.standupItem3: For each ticket row, record FE/BE/QA assignees (suggested from members), the update note, progress, and any blocker note. Rows save on blur.
helpSections.standupItem4: Per-assignee progress shows when a role name is filled; the ticket % mirrors their average. A Done ticket is always 100%.
helpSections.standupItem5: A blocker note auto-syncs into the Blockers panel; clearing it resolves the blocker.
helpSections.standupItem6: Group tickets by Epic or Parent/Story, filter by status (Done/Archived hidden by default), and toggle carry-over only.
helpSections.standupItem7: Keyboard: Enter / Alt+↑↓ move between rows; ⤢ expands a note into a popup.
helpSections.panelsItem1: Standup Lead — a rotating lead per working day (skips members on leave).
helpSections.panelsItem2: Team Members — position, leave status (+substitute), and story points per member.
helpSections.panelsItem3: Blockers — add/resolve (with a resolve note); synced from ticket blocker notes.
helpSections.panelsItem4: Update Log — every saved update (searchable, infinite scroll) with the note before→after.
helpSections.panelsItem5: Standup Duration Log — how long each standup took, who led, when.
helpSections.boardItem1: Live tickets of the board's active sprint: key (links to JIRA), type, status, priority, story points, assignee.
helpSections.boardItem2: Filter by status, group by epic/parent, carry-over only, ↻ Refresh to re-pull.
helpSections.clairvoyanceItem1: Read-only grooming view of upcoming work, live from JIRA. A source dropdown switches between each future (not-yet-started) sprint and the Backlog.
helpSections.clairvoyanceItem2: Each row: key (links to JIRA), type, summary, status, priority — grouped by parent/story.
helpSections.clairvoyanceItem3: If no future sprints or backlog exist yet, you'll see an empty state with a ↻ Reload button.
helpSections.tarotItem1: Estimate next-sprint tickets together — helps Sprint Planning. The landing page lists rooms (newest first); + Create room is top-right. Only one ACTIVE room per squad; others Join it instead of creating another.
helpSections.tarotItem2: The creator becomes Host; everyone else joins as a participant. The live roster (with online dots) updates in real time, and a sound plays when someone joins.
helpSections.tarotItem3: Host: pick a ticket and Start a session. Guests see the deck face-up and pick a card, then click it again to confirm. Cards reveal once everyone has voted, showing each pick, the team-synchronization %, and a suggested point (the most-picked value).
helpSections.tarotItem4: Host then Sets the story point (suggestion pre-filled) and fills per-role FE/BE/QA points (each capped at the ticket effort), or runs another cycle (Next cycle). If someone won't vote, the host can Reveal now (needs at least one confirmed card). An elapsed timer shows how long the round has run.
helpSections.tarotItem5: Reload-safe: if a guest refreshes mid-round their confirmed card is restored (not reset). The roster updates live as people join/leave — closing a tab drops you within a few seconds.
helpSections.tarotItem6: Special cards: ❓ = info unclear / needs discussion, ☕ = need a break. Hover a special card for its meaning.
helpSections.tarotItem7: Host controls: change the point scale (Fibonacci / Scrum / Custom, optionally set as squad default), Reset points (type RESET), End room (only when every ticket is pointed), Delete room (type DELETE), and kick participants.
helpSections.tarotItem8: Sync to Jira: once all tickets are pointed, write the effort + per-role points back to the squad's configured Jira fields (pick at least one). A failed ticket doesn't stop the rest — you're told how many synced and which failed. Reset Jira restores the values from before the last sync. Only a signed-in user (host or admin) can sync — guests cannot write to the board.
helpSections.tarotItem9: History: open an ended room any time to review its results (ticket links to Jira, title, effort + FE/BE/QA), grouped by parent/story, plus the attendance list. A host or admin can still Sync / Reset Jira from history. Ended rooms are auto-purged after a retention window (default 30 days) and can only be deleted manually by an admin.
helpSections.tarotItem10: Every key action (create, estimate, sync, reset, end) is recorded in the squad's Update Log.
helpSections.tarotItem11: Once the room is ended (or you're kicked) guests return to the Tarot landing with a thank-you. Ended rooms can only be deleted by an admin; their results stay as history.
helpSections.fortuneItem1: Signed-in members only (guests can't create JIRA tickets). Three modes: Single ticket, Epic breakdown, and Import (refine an existing ticket).
helpSections.fortuneItem2: Drop files (text, images, PDF) and/or type a requirement; pick a Gemini model (a recommended default is preselected). Gemini drafts a ticket in Gherkin GIVEN/WHEN/THEN UAC form.
helpSections.fortuneItem3: Not happy with the draft? Use Refine to send a follow-up prompt — Gemini rewrites it, keeping the original context.
helpSections.fortuneItem4: Before creating, a popup shows the target board (from the selected squad) and asks for the reporter's JIRA email (resolved to an accountId; if not found the ticket is still created and you're warned).
helpSections.fortuneItem5: Import mode: search a board ticket by key or title, edit + refine it, then Update JIRA. An Undo button restores the ticket's original values.
helpSections.fortuneItem6: Save drafts to continue later (update the open draft or save as a new one). History logs who generated/created/updated each ticket, with token usage and estimated cost.
helpSections.fortuneItem7: Recreate from History reopens an entry in the edit view first, so you can tweak before creating again.
helpSections.fortuneItem8: After creating: replace the [image-…] placeholders and TBD links (Figma/PRD/Postman/API) in the description, and complete the remaining JIRA fields (assignee, story points, sprint, components).
helpSections.previousSprintsItem1: Read-only history: sprint summary (status %, progress, blockers, man-power, story points), grouped tickets, per-ticket progress chart + update/blocker history.
helpSections.previousSprintsItem2: Export to Confluence (creates/updates one report page; export history is listed).
helpSections.velocityItem1: Story points per sprint (last 12): committed vs completed bars, plus the average completed velocity. Data comes from standup ticket snapshots (story points + status), so run standups to populate it. Hover bars/line for exact numbers.
helpSections.velocityItem2: Click a sprint bar to see its daily burndown — remaining points vs the ideal straight line across the sprint's calendar days. A ticket counts as completed once its status is Done/Closed/Resolved. A tips card offers guidance on healthy velocity.
helpSections.settingsItem1: Squads — add/switch/delete; edit name, board id, Story Point fields (default + FE/BE/QA; pick from the board field list), per-squad Confluence Space Key + Parent ID (blank = global env default), and the Tarot default point scale (Fibonacci/Scrum/Custom — a host can still override per room).
helpSections.settingsItem2: Members — add with a short name, optional full name, and position; edit or delete existing members inline. Plus leave (Annual/Sick/Permission + substitute), Sprints, Public Holidays, JIRA Board test.
helpSections.settingsItem3: Bulk setup: seed squads + members from JSON — in Settings → Bulk seed (paste or upload, download a template), or via `npm run db:seed:config` (idempotent).
helpSections.settingsItem4: Member JIRA account id (optional) — the opaque Atlassian id (e.g. 5b10ac8d82e05b22cc7d4ef5), not the email; links the member to their JIRA assignee. The field is a picker: choose a JIRA user (name + email) from the dropdown to fill the id, or paste one manually.
helpSections.settingsItem5: Admin Accounts — visible only to the environment super admin (the seeded SEED_ADMIN_EMAIL account). Add new admins (email + name + password), edit their name/email, reset their password, or delete them. Regular admins can use every other setting but cannot manage admin accounts. The super admin row is read-only and cannot be edited or deleted (prevents lockout).
helpSections.settingsItem6: Danger Zone — reset the database (admin only).
helpSections.standupItem8: Hold — mark a ticket on hold (blocked / deprioritized) with the Hold button; the row greys out. Resume clears it. It's a visual cue saved per date; it doesn't change points or progress.
helpSections.moonPhaseItem1: Each member on the Stand Up Meeting team list has a mood emoji (scale 1–5, default 😄). Only the chosen emoji shows.
helpSections.moonPhaseItem2: Click or hover the emoji to open the picker; hover any mood to read what it represents, then click to set it.
helpSections.moonPhaseItem3: Mood can only be set while a standup is running. Start Standup seeds a default 😄 for everyone that day; End Standup locks the pickers until it is started again.
helpSections.moonPhaseItem4: The Moon Phase page (under 🔮 Crystal Ball) charts team mood over the sprint and per member for days a standup was started, and lets you browse previous sprints.

tarot.pageSubtitle: Estimate next-sprint tickets together. Helps Sprint Planning.
tarot.activeRoomExistsTitle: An active room already exists for this squad
tarot.activeRoomBannerAfter: is currently active. Join it instead of creating a new one.
tarot.noRoomsYet: No rooms yet. Create one to start estimating.
tarot.removedByHost: You were removed from the room by the host.
tarot.noEstimatedTickets: No estimated tickets in this session.
tarot.waitingForHost: Waiting for the host to start a session…
tarot.cardLockedWaiting: Card locked. Waiting for everyone else…
tarot.pickCardHint: Pick a card, then click it again to confirm.
tarot.noTicketsNextSprint: No tickets in the next sprint.
tarot.kickConfirmDesc: Remove {{name}} from the room? They can rejoin unless you end the session.
tarot.resetJiraSyncDesc: Restore each ticket's Jira fields to the values captured before the last sync. This writes to the Jira board.
tarot.resetAllPointsDesc: This clears every decided point in this room. Type RESET to confirm.
tarot.deleteRoomDesc: This permanently deletes the room and its history. Type DELETE to confirm.
tarot.endedHistoryNote: Session ended · history. Ended rooms can only be deleted by an admin.
tarot.perRoleHint: Per-role points cannot exceed the ticket effort.
tarot.syncFieldsHint: Pick which point fields to write to Jira (mapped to this squad's configured fields). At least one required.
tarot.restoreJiraValuesTitle: Restore Jira values from before the last sync
tarot.tipGuest1Body: Open the room link (or Join the active room from this page). You appear in the roster with an online dot. No account needed — a guest name is enough.
tarot.tipGuest2Body: When the host starts a ticket, the deck turns face-up. Tap the card matching your estimate. It lifts to show it's selected — nobody sees your value yet.
tarot.tipGuest3Body: Tap your selected card again to confirm (it locks in). Everyone's cards stay hidden until all online voters have confirmed, so you can't be anchored by others.
tarot.tipGuest4Body: Pick ❓ when the ticket is unclear and needs discussion, or ☕ when you need a break. Hover any special card to see its meaning. Reloading keeps your confirmed card.
tarot.tipHost1Body: As host, pick a next-sprint ticket and Start a session. That flips the deck for everyone. Walk the team through the ticket first so estimates are informed.
tarot.tipHost2Body: Cards auto-reveal once every online voter confirms. If someone stalls, use Reveal now (needs ≥1 confirmed). The reveal shows each pick, the team-sync %, and a suggested point (most-picked).
tarot.tipHost3Body: Set the story point (suggestion pre-filled) and per-role FE/BE/QA points (each ≤ effort). Big spread? Discuss the outliers and run Next cycle. Change the scale (Fibonacci/Scrum/Custom) if needed.
tarot.tipHost4Body: End the room once every ticket is pointed. Then Sync to Jira writes effort + per-role points to the squad's fields (reversible via Reset Jira). Guests can't sync — only a signed-in host/admin.
tarot.guestNoSync: Sign in to sync to Jira
tarot.guestNoSyncHint: Jira sync needs a logged-in account (host or admin). Guests can run the session but can't write to the Jira board.

mood.disabledHint: Start the standup to set moods

moonPhase.heading: Moon Phase
moonPhase.subtitle: Team mood through the sprint — and how it has waxed and waned over past sprints.
moonPhase.dummyNotice: Preview with sample data — mood tracking is not wired to the backend yet.
moonPhase.tip1Body: A dipping curve often precedes a blocker or burnout — check in before it bottoms out.
moonPhase.tip2Body: Use a low mood as a reason to ask, not a verdict. Context beats the number.
moonPhase.tip3Body: One bad day is noise; three in a row is a pattern worth acting on.
moonPhase.tip4Body: When the team glows, name what worked so you can repeat it next sprint.
moonPhase.noData: No mood data yet. Set moods on the Stand Up Meeting page to see them here.
moonPhase.noSprintData: No moods recorded for this sprint yet.
